parsed_review.summary: CP00-917 is a descriptor-only RP30 P02 bridge (RP30.P02.M09.S06-RP30.P02.M10.S03) carrying the RecruitmentWorkflow tail into RiskWorkflow re-entry rows. The pack-level descriptor-only production_ready claim (flag hrx_recruitment_risk_workflow_tail_bridge_descriptor_verified) is fully supported by source inspection. registry.js:4457 (createHrxCp917RecruitmentRiskWorkflowBridgeDescriptor) and registry.js:1565-1695 (createHrxDomainRows) build deep-frozen evidence rows where every recruitment/risk runtime, decision-write, record-write, candidate/risk-event payload, offer-creation, and risk-score flag is false; the descriptor spreads HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and HRX_BLOCKED_CLAIMS. HRX_CP917_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:423-438) and HRX_CP917_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:1480-1514) match the baseline exactly: M09 S06-S12 (7 RecruitmentWorkflow) + M10 S01-S03 (3 RiskWorkflow) = 10 units; deliverable {implementation:10}; micro_title {RecruitmentWorkflow:7, RiskWorkflow:3}. validators.js:3484-3681 enforces all boundaries with strict !==false/!==true checks (no-real-data, no-secret, no-separate-product, Claude-not-final-approver, Hermes emits_runtime_receipt:false) including per-section M09 RecruitmentWorkflow and M10 RiskWorkflow descriptor checks and contract projection. Tests at model.test.js:1121-1196 assert binding, coverage, descriptor closure, and contract projection (current/latest CP00-917, latest_projection HrxCp917RecruitmentRiskWorkflowBridgeDescriptor, validateHrxPeopleContract errors==[]). The validate script (scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs) chains the full CP897->CP917 binding lineage plus coverage, descriptor, contract, capabilities, and safety gates; the contract (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json:25090-25092) carries validation.valid:true with a clean no_write_attestation and CP917 capabilities/safety gates. A structural scan found the only import in packages/hrx/src is validators.js->registry.js, with no node:fs/child_process/http/net/process anywhere, confirming the no-runtime/no-write attestation beyond the boolean flags. All 6 commands in command-evidence.json exit 0 and every referenced npm script (targeted and the deferred deterministic ladder) exists in package.json. Authority boundary is intact (claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true) and Hermes is used only as a deterministic evidence packet. The pack correctly reports production_ready:false / pending_review with no premature claim, readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1444 bytes), and no prior invalid review attempts are laundered as valid evidence (invalid_review_attempts empty). Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, workbook docs, LDIP result, docx) are correctly excluded per changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note records that the full deterministic validation ladder and adjudication remain correctly gated after this review under non-Claude authority. This review does not block pack or CP-goal closeout.

findings.title: production_ready promotion correctly remains gated on post-review deterministic ladder and adjudication
findings.details: The pack honestly reports production_ready:false and status in_progress/pending_review (manifest.json:5-6, claude-review-result.json:4, construction-inspection.json:5-9, adjudication.md). command-evidence.json:50-52 documents that only the targeted HRX checks (node --check syntax, node --test model tests, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) have run and exit 0, while the full deterministic validation ladder (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is intentionally deferred until review normalization and adjudication. This is the expected sequencing, not a defect: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver (review-request.json:8-13, descriptor authority_boundary at registry.js:4464-4469), and all referenced npm scripts exist in package.json.
findings.recommendation: After this PASS review is recorded, the non-Claude (Codex/human) authority must run the full deterministic validation ladder, complete adjudication and queue regeneration, and only then flip production_ready and the hrx_recruitment_risk_workflow_tail_bridge_descriptor_verified flag. No code change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_only_non_blocking; expected post-review sequencing confirmed; does not block pack or goal closeout
